title: Coruscant of the Outer Rim
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Outer Rim}}
'''Coruscant of the Outer Rim''' was a title given to several [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city worlds]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] and other regions in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], which could rival the urban sprawl and growth of the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. One notable contender was [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], which earned the title in 4300 BBY, until trade lanes shifted in 4156 BBY, where it then suffered two hundred years of economic decline, before being [[Destruction of Taris|razed to the ground]] in [[3956 BBY]] by a [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] under [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Malak|Malak]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. The heavily urbanized trade center of [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] in the heart of the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]] between the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] and the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] also aspired to achieve this title.

By extension [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] and its sister world [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]], both also in the Outer Rim, had rivaled Coruscant economically. There were also several more urban worlds across the galaxy that rivaled the galactic capital politically, including [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] and to a lesser extent its [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] of the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] and [[Denon/Legends|Denon]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].

==Appearances==
